text:
I can’t wait to welcome you into the most fun, silly, sweet, wine filled community @samsommwineclub!!!🎉🍷🍾😻💛🍹🥂
hope you can treat yourself to a midweek micro joy 🫶🏼 

✨ We meet virtually every month to explore new wines, regions, & winemakers (with special guests too)! There are East & West Coast tastings so everyone can join. I offer FOUR LIVE tastings every single month!!!

💻 Can’t make it live? Every tasting is recorded to watch anytime!

🍇 Wine is totally optional! I personally help every member find great alternatives based on your budget & location.

📲 You get 24/7 access to me for any wine questions via my “pocket somm” feature!

✈️ We also do in-person trips! Just got back from Paso Robles CA, upcoming Provence 🇫🇷, Santa Barbra, and Pacific Northwest!!!

📚 You’ll also get access to hundreds exclusive wine / travel guides + the most supportive & fun wine-loving community ever 🫶🏼

DM ME FOR A DISCOUNT CODE!!!!

#traderjoesfoodreviews #traderjoeshaul #winetiktok🍷 @Trader Joe's

fun fact, I am an 805 simi valley girly born and raised, I grew up going to San Luis Obispo every year…☀️🫶🏼 it’s truly one of the most magical hidden gem food and wine regions!!! I am so thankful for all the friends and memz i’ve made here through the years!!! I recently went back to be the no snob somm at the first ever @slowinemixer and was able to carve out some fun shenanigans thanks to @shareslo 🥂🥰

🛌 hotel stay was @thehotelslo and I can’t recommend it enough!!! 

🍷places I wine tasted this trip were @biddleranchvineyard and @tolosawinery !! I recommend both and if you go let me know and I have quite a few more for you to add to your itinerary!! ;) 

🍽️ places we dined: @misturarestaurantebar , @linnaeascafe , @lincolnmarketdeli , @natesonmarsh , @oldslobbq , @tasteseeds , @kreuzbergcalifornia 

🍸places we drank @drinkregion @highbar_slo 

🖼️ places we shopped & explored @slomuseumofart @thrifty.beaches @understory.plantshop @ahlouisstore @booboorecords 

until we meet again …. 💕💕💕💕

#shareslo @ShareSLO
wine: 🥰 🛌 hotel stay was @thehotelslo and I can’t recommend it enough!!!
drinkName: 🥰 🛌 hotel stay was @thehotelslo and I can’t recommend it enough!!!

text:
no I don’t want a f*cking matcha date I wanna drink sauvy b slushies and eat tbell on the porch with you!!!

⬇️ SAUVY B WINE SLUSHIE ReCiPe ⬇️

- handful frozen mango
- a few frozen peaches 
- big pour of your favorite Sauvy B, i am using @dutchercrossing in this recipe 
- big squeeze of spicy honey to your liking (more honey for sweeter slushie) 
- splash of Gin (I love @empress1908gin )
- Ice
- sprinkle of Tajín 
blend together & chill 

@tacobell 

#virtualwinetasting #sauvyb #tbell #slushie #sauvybsummer

your no snob somm prescribing: one campari spritz 🍊

Recipe ⬇️

Ingredient

- Ice
- 1-2 ounces Campari ( bitter orange 🍊 liqueur )
- Prosecco
- Fresh orange 

Steps

Pour Campari over ice, squeeze orange, top with Prosecco. I love using @lamarcaprosecco@La Marca Prosecco 🍾

#camparispritz #campari #sommelier

text: #onthisday

family recipes taste better with wine. It’s science. hope this inspires you to recreate a dish that reminds you of someone you love or miss 🩷🫶🏼

Grandma Rosemary’s Beef Stroganoff x @erinnobrienn lightened up version: 

INGREDIENTS
2 corn starch 
I tsp. salt
1/4 tsp. pepper
I lb. ground beef 
3 garlic clove, minced
1 cup half and half 
1/2 cup onion, chopped
2 1/2 cups beef broth 
I can cream of chicken soup (optional. I didn’t use it and it’s still very creamy. I added 1/4 cup parmesan at the end)
I lb. mushrooms (optional) 
I cup sour cream 
parsley for garnish 

INSTRUCTIONS:
melt butter or avocado oil and cook beef until almost cooked through. Add onion and garlic. Whisk broth mixture and add to pan. Add egg noodles and bring to a boil. Reduce head to low and simmer through (8 min). Remove from heat and add sour cream and parmesan. Top with parsley! 

WINE PAIRING:
🍷 @maisonjosephdrouhin Bourgogne (Pinot Noir)
wine: Maison Joseph Drouhin Bourgogne Pinot Noir
drinkName: Maison Joseph Drouhin Bourgogne Pinot Noir

#onthisday

I’ve always been honest about living with anxiety while working in the wine industry, and regularly enjoying wine myself.

My space isn’t for extremists. It’s for nuance.

I’ve struggled with anxiety since I was 13.
Wine didn’t create that.
And truthfully? I’m doing WAY better now than I was back then.

Wine brings me joy.
It’s part of how I connect, create, gather, and celebrate.
It helped build the life and career I have today.

Do I think balance matters? Of course.
Do I work on it intentionally? Always.

But my life would feel smaller without wine in it.

If you’ve ever felt societal guilt around enjoying a glass… I hope you feel safe here.

And if you’re craving a silly, cozy, thoughtful space to enjoy wine without shame, my Sam Somm Wine Club was made exactly for that. Once a month. Friends. Zero judgment. Just good bottles and good energy. ✨💘🍷

mercury is in retrograde so it’s Taco Bell & wine season, we WILL survive!!! 🙂‍↔️

Current fave pairings: 

🍷🌮 Cheesy Gordita Crunch + Lambrusco , there’s one at TJ’s called Grotte Reggiano Lambrusco Dolce for less than $6 and it’s elite 

🍷🌮 Spicy Potato Taco + Rosé , try @lavieilleferme aka the chicken wine for a budget friendly option you can find almost everywhere! 

🍷🌮 Beefy 5 layer burrito + Sangiovese , try one of my favorite small production Paso Robles Sangiovese from @highcampwines and thank me later!

🍷🌮 Doritos Locos Taco + Zinfandel, look for OZV which is a budget friendly Zin found at most grocery stores and wine retailers! 

🍷🌮 Chicken Quesadilla + Grüner Veltliner , try @ulloacellars GV for one of the most magic crisp white wines you’ll ever experience ✨ 

PSA! if you love my approach to wine here on IG and you’re ready to take our relationship to the next level.
you’re going to LOVE the @samsommwineclub 🎉🥰

✨endless laughs + sips
✨t bell & unhinged couch crusher pairing events 
✨tasting with hidden gem winemakers 
✨an affordable date night to yourself every month
✨me as your 24/7 personal sommelier 
✨meet & connect with silly, fun wine lovers across the globe

it’s chaotic. it’s cozy. it’s elevated but never pretentious.
come sip on the couch with us!!! comment below if you’re seriously interested in trying out my silly virtual wine community and i’ll send you a DM! 🙂‍↔️
wine: Lambrusco / Rosé / Sangiovese / Zinfandel / Grüner Veltliner
drinkName: Lambrusco / Rosé / Sangiovese / Zinfandel / Grüner Veltliner

text: solo dates that include tacobell, movies that wreck you, and cocktails are truly good for the soul. I can attest! what did the last solo date you did for yourself include? i’d love some more inspiration to do this again  ᵕ̈  @kaitlynbristowe reminded me how much I needed this!!🙏🏼

❤️10 years of Sam Somm!!!!❤️One of the most common questions I get is: how did I get into wine? ⬇️

And honestly… it wasn’t because I loved wine.

When I first started, I didn’t really know what I wanted from the wine industry. I just knew I wanted a career that brought me joy, and hopefully something I could be good at.

Now, 10 years later, I’ve had time to reflect on what I love most about my job.

Yes, I love wine. I have a deep appreciation for it and the humans behind every bottle.

But what I love most… is seeing people happy.
Seeing people laugh.
Seeing people come together and do something fun for themselves.
To feel seen. To feel loved. To feel supported.

Wine is just the common denominator. 🍷

For the last 10 years, I’ve never let go of my mission to keep wine fun and approachable for any level of wine drinker.

It all started with me leading a wine tasting for a bachelorette party… and the rest was history.

Since then, I’ve led thousands of wine tasting experiences for fun-loving winos across the globe, started my no-fuss wine label @faffwine, built an online wine club community @samsommwineclub, and made friends for life along the way. ❤️

The thing that inspires me most is seeing people share wine with the people they love and not take ourselves too seriously.

Thank you for trusting me as your no-snob somm. 🫶🏼
instead of having a mental (and physical) breakdown, I decided to make and drink this. i’m healed and couldn’t gatekeep. share with your friend who needs this. your no snob somm’s orders ;)

text:
for anyone feeling guilty for not partaking in “dry january” here’s a reminder that two things can be true at once:

we can use wine to cope with anxiety and that can sometimes lead to over indulgence/ more anxiety...
However, we can genuinely love and appreciate wine and want to enjoy it not just because it’s alcohol. 

We love the taste, how it pairs with food, the cultural experience, the travel, and the story, behind it!

it’s okay for both things to be true! we are only human. always do what’s best for you!❤️ with that being said, i’ll be drinking all January ;)

Xo
